title: Stochastic Path Sampler For Lattice Field Theory
authors: Shiyang Chen; Moxian Qian; Gert Aarts; Biagio Lucini; Kai Zhou
problem: Sample an unnormalized lattice target near criticality without target training data while controlling long autocorrelation.
method: Learn forward and auxiliary backward Langevin paths with a path-space KL objective, then correct independent paths with trajectory-level IMH.
key_equation: D_KL = integral dq_F log(dq_F / d qtilde_B)
experiment: 2D Lx8 phi4; L=16,32,48,64; lambda=0.022; kappa=0.20-0.30; HMC baseline
headline_result: Corrected observables agree with the tested HMC benchmark and the reported absolute-magnetization autocorrelation is shorter near the finite-volume pseudocritical region.
author_limitation: Autocorrelation units differ, total cost is not matched, and scaling beyond the tested 2D setup remains open.
reviewer_inference: The evidence does not establish a universal wall-time or GPU-hour speedup.
safe_sentence: In the tested 2D benchmark, SPS+IMH reproduces the reported HMC observables while a matched total-cost comparison remains open.
prohibited_sentence: SPS is universally 320 times faster than HMC.
